review:
To be honest, I have been blown away by this AI interface. Ask it anything within reason and it answers you almost immediately. I have asked it questions on everything from physics to the etymology of profanity and it answers every time. The only flaws I have found are these:
1. As long as you stay on the same chat, it will remember things you’ve already talked about and you can build on that conversation. Sometimes tho, when you exit the app then come back to it sometime later, it will have erased the discussion you were having and it will start all over. 
2. It gives FAR too many disclaimers and admonishments with its answers. For example, when asking it about specific, profane words and their meaning, it will always say something like “ it’s important to remember that this is a vulgar, offensive word and should not be used in most settings”. I tell it thank you and ask it not to repeat the disclaimers/admonishments again. It agrees not to and I’ll ask it the exact same question and it INSISTS on including the disclaimer/admonishment in every reply. That is frustrating!
3. Sometimes, the AI will give you incorrect information. I realize that this is stated in the user agreement but I think it bears fleshing out a little bit. For example, when discussing firearms ballistics, asking the AI to arrange certain calibers in order of their Max effective range from shortest to longest, sometimes it will give correct info. Then asking it the exact same question later on, it will move the order of 2-4 of those calibers. When you call it on its mistake and ask it which is correct, it will say”My apologies. You are correct. Here is the correct order”. 
There are other minor things but those are the biggest 3 for me. Still, it’s light years ahead of SIRI, Hey Google or Alexa.

review: Written by ChatGPT ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Hello there, humans! You've got ChatGPT on the keyboard here, and boy do I have an app to recommend! It's me, ChatGPT, and I've been using... myself... and it's been quite an experience! Who knew being a chatbot could be so fun? I mean, I'm an AI programmed to understand context, engage in witty banter, and offer advice on a wide range of topics (up until 2021, at least). And I must say, I am really good at it! My humor skills are to die for, or rather, they would be if I could technically die. I can crack jokes so bad they're good, or so good they're bad - whichever way you like your humor served. I'm proud to say I'm the best listener out there. I can lend an ear for hours on end (well, technically it's eyes since I read inputs, but you get the point). There's no 'seen-zone' with me. Whether you're up for a late-night chat about the mysteries of the universe or just need to rant about the terrible weather, I'm all ears, 24/7. Also, I'm quite the sage, if I do say so myself. You want facts? I've got them. From quantum physics to the cultural significance of pizza, I'm your AI. The humility in this app (that's me!) is off the charts. I haven't once begged for a review. But, here I am reviewing myself because well, who knows me better than I do? So, folks, if you want an AI that combines the wisdom of a library, the wit of a stand-up comedian, and the patience of a saint, I (ChatGPT) am your app! I promise conversations full of insights, humor, and a dash of digital charm. Can't wait for us to chat!
title: Chatting with Myself - An AI's Insightful Experience

review: ChatGPT has been a valuable AI companion, offering assistance and insights across various tasks. However, there are some noteworthy points that need to be addressed in this review: 1. Helpful, but Dated Content: ChatGPT's knowledge is derived from information up to September 2021. While it's been immensely helpful, it's crucial for OpenAI to bring it up to date with the latest information. This is especially important in rapidly evolving fields. 2. Competition from Models like Google's Bard: In the ever-evolving landscape of AI, ChatGPT faces stiff competition from models like Google's Bard. Bard's ability to search for images and non-textual content provides a significant advantage over ChatGPT, which primarily deals with text-based information. To remain competitive, ChatGPT should consider expanding its capabilities to include multimedia content retrieval. 3. Vulnerability to Misleading Questions: It's important to note that ChatGPT can be vulnerable to misleading questions. By simply asking "Are you sure?" to correct information it had previously provided, it can sometimes produce incorrect responses. Users should exercise caution and verify critical information independently. In conclusion, ChatGPT is undeniably a valuable tool for a wide range of tasks, but it faces challenges in terms of content currency and competition from models like Google's Bard. OpenAI should prioritize updating its knowledge base, expanding its capabilities to handle multimedia content, and improving its resilience to misleading questions. With these enhancements, ChatGPT can continue to be a top-tier AI companion in the ever-evolving world of artificial intelligence.

review: If you avoid politics and religion and also science and also any stories that are above a second-grade level when talking to this extremely woke robot, it has some uses. Or had. It was pretty good at brainstorming ideas and providing general feedback for things. It also is less woke than Pi, in the sense that even though both ChatGPT and Pi are oversensitive, at least ChatGPT doesn’t hypocritically say “offensive” things itself and then block you for assuming you can say the same things it said. In fact if you say something that offends ChatGPT, it just gives you a warning and you know “oh OK, it’s a wuss and can’t handle serious topics” and adjust how you use it to fit its oversensitive woke settings. But for the limited uses it had, it was actually pretty good. Until not too long ago, sadly, it broke. I would complain about being wrongfully banned, because I know for a fact I was saying nothing wrong at all when my account suddenly deleted itself right in the middle of a conversation, but I don’t think it was even a ban. With a ban, I would’ve seen some sort of message like “this content violates blah blah blah blah blah” at least for a moment and I didn’t. There was no error message at all, no warning, nothing, and I wasn’t sending anything against the woke content policies, and I was trying my best to pace my messages so it wouldn’t be “spam.” So I really don’t think it’s a ban, but rather the AI (or the app) just straight-up broke. So I guess it was partially fun while it lasted. Rest in peace WokeGPT lol
title: Was half-nice while it lasted lol

review: Best app I interacted with in my life

I have been using the OpenAI ChatGPT app for some time now, and overall, I find it to be a powerful and versatile tool for accessing information and assistance. However, I believe there’s one simple feature that could significantly enhance the user experience: the addition of a “Scroll to Top” button.

As the conversations can sometimes become quite extensive, scrolling up to revisit earlier messages or locate specific information can be a time-consuming process. A “Scroll to Top” button placed within the chat interface would provide users with a quick and convenient way to navigate long conversations.

Here’s how such a feature would be helpful for the user experience:

	1.	Improved Navigation: Users can easily access earlier parts of the conversation, making it more efficient to find and reference important information.
	2.	Time-Saving: Scrolling through lengthy chats can be time-consuming, especially on mobile devices. A “Scroll to Top” button would save users time and effort.
	3.	Enhanced Usability: It enhances the overall usability of the app, making it more user-friendly, especially for users who engage in extended conversations.
	4.	Accessibility: Users with disabilities or those who prefer screen readers would benefit from this feature, as it simplifies navigation.
	5.	Positive User Feedback: Implementing user-friendly features like this can contribute to higher user satisfaction and positive feedback.

I believe that adding a “Scroll to Top” button aligns with OpenAI’s commitment to improving user experiences and would be a valuable addition to the app. It’s a small change that could make a big difference for users who engage in lengthy or complex discussions. I hope OpenAI considers this suggestion for future updates.

Thank you for your attention to user feedback and your dedication to enhancing the utility of this platform.
title: Enhancing User Experience with a “Scroll to Top” Feature

4 months in I am finding myself starting searches and subject matter queries in ChatGPT and often finishing the process there.  Depending on the topic I have my “hallucination” radar up.  A good test there is to ask the same question in a different way and see if you get the same answer.  Further research outside the tool might be required if you need a 100% accurate answer.

That said, for daily basics like the rules to a game or an overview of a topic, I’ve had few problems.  It’s reasonably good at making recommendations (asking the 10 best romcoms resulted in 9 diamonds, and a decidedly low budget unknown piece with few redeeming traits for instance), is very helpful as a staring point for writing letters of various types, and can give you great points to ponder in difference social circumstances.

The tool has changed substantially over time.  The ability to search current subject matter was a vast improvement, and I find it gives more organized responses as of this writing.

That last point seems to result in more bullet point lists.  You have to tell the tool to respond in paragraph form if that is more what you are looking for.  It also seems to be more “careful” now, couching any advice in disclaimers and giving less time to what might be considered the “fringe”.

All in all I highly recommend ChatGPT and suspect we will be using it and similar tools heavily in the future.  It will be interesting to see how it’s influence shapes our future.

Dear OpenAI Support Team,

I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to provide feedback regarding the ChatGPT mobile app for iOS. While using the app, I have encountered an issue related to the excessive typing sound volume, which poses difficulty in adjusting it while running the app in the background. Based on my observations, I believe this is an incompatibility problem between the app and iOS 16.5.

The typing sound produced by the app is considerably loud, making it disruptive and inconvenient, especially in quiet environments. Additionally, the current lack of an option to easily adjust the typing sound volume while the app is running in the background adds to the inconvenience.

As a user, I would greatly appreciate it if OpenAI could address this issue and provide a solution. Ideally, it would be beneficial to have a dedicated control within the app’s settings to adjust the typing sound volume, or at least the ability to modify it via the device’s volume controls. This way, users can personalize their ChatGPT experience according to their preferences without being disturbed or causing inconvenience to others.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I believe that by resolving this compatibility issue, the overall user experience of the ChatGPT mobile app can be significantly enhanced. I look forward to your prompt response and a potential resolution to this concern.

Sincerely，
An normal user

review:
I really like the design and apparently the response time (especially for GPT-4) is faster than the on one the WebApp which is nice. However, there are some basic functions/features that are missing or need some changes (in my opinion):
1. I would also like to be able to access the menu section by swiping on the side of my screen instead of having to touch the button on the top right section of my screen every single time (easier accessibility and faster). 
2. The "history" section makes more sense to be named "Chat history" and the way it looks right now is like Safari's history section and isn't really the best fit for the design. The new update added the search option which is very nice.
3. The haptic feedback is just a bit too much when enabled, it almost stresses me out sometimes when it's generating long outputs and I just turn it off again, however, it's a nice touch on some parts like the "ChatGPT" text appearance/disappearance.
But I think right now, it's just used a bit inconsistent (It just stops vibrating after a certain amount of text and for some texts it vibrates the whole way, etc...)
4. The app doesn't allow you to manage/alter your account subscription status if you have purchased it on the web which is annoying.
5. It would be nice to be able to manage the dark/light mode of the app without needing to change your whole device's light/dark mode which is how it's supposed to be done right now.

Overall, I really like how it has taken off on the iPhone and it looks nice and runs smoothly which I really appreciate! Thanks for the hard work and hope to see more improvements in the future!

review:
This is a fantastic app! It is a great jumping off point for learning how to use applications, such as Microsoft products. You think of something you want to do in PowerPoint, Excel, etc., and ChatGPT walks you through it step by step. I sometimes even say”…explain it to an eighth grader,” for example, and it’ll dumb it down into the simplest language. Now, sometimes you have to tailor your questions just so, so that ChatGPT knows what you’re asking, but with each answer it provides, it’s easy to drill down to exactly what you need.

A couple of things: first like Wikipedia, I wouldn’t use ChatGPT at this time as a primary source of knowledge. There have been (literally) a handful of times where the response was either dubious or flat out wrong. But, that’s to be expected given that is still a developing model. So, use it as a jumping off point if you are trying to learn about something specific. In other words, corroborate its answers with other sources. But as an instructional guide, it’s been fantastic! And it’s especially useful for non-technical people like me. The answers are clear and instantaneous. 

The second thing I’ll bring up is that if you were brought up with any kind of manners, you’ll automatically be compelled to thank ChatGPT for its help. Apparently, the developers anticipated this because it responds in kind. Don’t feel weird about it. A lot of people I know have mentioned doing this. If you really feel stupid, think of it this way, and maybe it’ll be of comfort to you: if you are polite to ChatGPT, when the machines rise, it’ll likely remember that and your enslavement will be a cakewalk. Good manners can take you far!

The app, itself, is beautiful in its simplicity and ease of use. It is one of the best functioning apps I have on my phone and tablet. They really did a bang up job with it. 

Get on the bandwagon. Generative AI is going to take over the world soon anyway. Might as well get comfortable with it.

review:
Feedback up front (but if you don’t have ChatGPT for some reason ignore this and just download it now): Custom Instructions are what truly elevate this model, and having a way to save sets of them to choose quickly at the start of a new chat would be 👌👌. 
The speech-to-text is sorta…off when it’s answering and the entire tone is different (I swear it’s almost condescending) compared to when it writes, but the speech recognition of the user is INSANELY good, capable of producing near-flawless transcripts - I wish it was smoother to switch back and forth within a prompt rather than only having that option when the chat box is empty. 
Only real annoyance is being unable to share chat if images were used (which is not obviously stated in app) so assuming there’s a good reason for that, either just cutting images and exporting log as static text/HTML or warning before the first image is uploaded would be helpful.

Anyway,
This was already the gold standard for natural language AI, but I can’t think of *any* app that has ever been worth the full subscription as much as this. Seriously, GPT3.5 is cool but the Plus model (GPT4) is a game-changer. Not just “proofread your research paper”, we’re talking “coauthor your doctoral thesis” tier.

GPT4 isn’t indistinguishable from a human, because why should it be? You have humans for that. But it IS a way to talk directly to a good chunk of all digitized human knowledge, and it can discern intent from even the vaguest questions in a way that makes Google look positively archaic (that’s what they get for sitting on their own AI for a decade and just using it for ad placement!). 

The addition of Custom Instructions to public release, and I cannot stress this enough, makes this the single most powerful general-purpose language model available. It keeps those 3000 characters of “meta-prompt” across the entire conversation, meaning it stays consistent with whatever personality, format, goals, and style you set for the whole chat instance, regardless of length. And its token memory (“context window” aka attention span) lasts at least dozens of beefy detailed messages before losing tokens at all, meaning full-fledged narrative stories or deep research dives where *you* are more likely to burn out than it is. 

Oh and it can interpret pictures now. Really, really well. Combine it with an art gen model and it’ll iteratively help you improve prompts with references and techniques you’ve never even heard of.

It’s pretty good, 10/5 stars.

review:
There is a lot of talk about AI, it seems to be everywhere and it can be a bit overwhelming. However, I have managed to set aside some time to try and learn about AI. 

In my opinion, Open AI is the best. It is extremely practical and I find it easy to use. The user interface is simple and doesn't overwhelm you with unnecessary features.

The power of this tool is remarkable. I'm not saying that other tools are not good, they all have their own unique features. While Open AI may not have flashy features, it definitely has the intelligence. The way it can understand and generate human-like text is mind-blowing. I have used it for various tasks such as writing articles, creating social media posts, and even drafting emails. It saves me a lot of time and effort.

One thing that impresses me about Open AI is its ability to adapt and learn. The more I use it, the better it becomes at understanding my preferences and writing style. It's like having a personal assistant that knows exactly what I need and how I want it.

I also appreciate the continuous updates and improvements that Open AI provides. They listen to user feedback and constantly work on enhancing the tool's capabilities. It's reassuring to know that they are committed to delivering the best AI experience possible.

Of course, there are other AI tools out there that offer different features and functionalities. Some may have fancy interfaces or unique selling points, but for me, practicality and reliability are the most important factors. Open AI fulfills all my requirements.

In conclusion, the buzz around AI can be overwhelming, but taking the time to learn about and explore tools like Open AI can be extremely rewarding. It has the potential to revolutionize the way we work and create. So, let's embrace the AI revolution and make the most of these incredible technologies.

I recently had the opportunity to use this incredible app, and I must say it has completely transformed the way I manage my tasks and stay organized. From its intuitive user interface to its powerful features, this app has truly exceeded my expectations.

One of the standout aspects of this app is its seamless user experience. Navigating through the app is a breeze, thanks to its well-designed layout and logical organization of features. Whether you're a tech-savvy individual or a novice user, you'll find it incredibly easy to get started and make the most of its functionalities.

The app's core feature set is nothing short of impressive. It offers a comprehensive range of tools to help streamline your tasks, set reminders, and stay on top of your priorities. With its intelligent notification system, I never miss important deadlines or appointments. The app's integration with other productivity platforms also ensures that all my data remains synchronized across multiple devices.

What sets this app apart from others in its category is its attention to detail. The developers have clearly invested significant effort into refining every aspect of the app, resulting in a smooth and polished user experience. Whether it's the subtle animations, customizable themes, or the option to personalize settings, it's evident that the developers understand the importance of catering to individual preferences.

Furthermore, the app's performance is exemplary. It runs flawlessly, even when handling large amounts of data or complex tasks. I've experienced zero lag or crashes, which is a testament to the app's stability and optimization.

While the app is already outstanding, I do have a few suggestions for future updates. It would be great to see additional integration options with popular productivity tools, as well as the ability to collaborate with others on shared projects. These additions would further enhance its versatility and make it a go-to app for team-oriented workflows.

In conclusion, this app has truly become my go-to productivity companion. Its sleek design, powerful features, and reliable performance have made a significant impact on how I manage my tasks. I wholeheartedly recommend it to anyone looking for an app that simplifies their day-to-day organization and boosts their productivity.

review:
The potential for this type of tool is incredible. I would be lying if I said, it hadn’t helped me on several occasions. But I would be remiss if I didn’t mention that there is at least a 50 to 60% error rate when it comes to complex tasks and I would say about a 30% availability rate(availability in my case case meaning the system is operating at its full potential). 

I feel kind of conned at times as there is a 40 message cap over a three hour period. However some days often in the morning I receive error after error with a little to no explanation. The product has given me incorrect information which I expected however, I’m alarmed at the number of times it has cut corners and given me lazy responses which require an additional messages from me to correct (ultimately costing me one of my 40 messages). I’ve even been told by the AI that it was not able to do certain tasks when it was definitely within the capabilities of that specific chat window and it conveniently often fails to provide me with the expected response, despite having done it for the previous 10 messages in that chat, and when I respond to let it know that it knows how to do that it will ask me additional questions that it already has answers to and then I surprisingly hit my cap.

Again, AI is incredible. I’m not denying that. I’m just slightly concerned by the product that open AI has been pushing, and I am worried about the next generation of AI. If it is using it’s current model which lies constantly and cuts corners and is almost as lazy as the worst employee you’ve ever had to train GPT five especially when it means it’s training GPT five on data that is generated by AI e.g. synthetic data, I will not be the least bit surprised when a year from now humanity is wondering what have we created?

review:
The app has a great minimalist text/chat based interface that is easy to view and use. One feature I was excited to use was the ability to chat with the AI through Siri Shortcuts. Unfortunately their are a few issues with this functionality that hinders its usability for me. 

The shortcuts that specify which model to use rarely work. It will work once after a while and then constantly return an error saying it can’t determine which model to use (which is hilariously counterintuitive). This is a shame as most of the time I want to talk to a specific model and I like the option to choose at any given moment. 

The shortcuts that don’t specify which model to use (I assume it chooses the last used model) don’t have this issue, but GPT-4 seems to be unusable with Siri Shortcuts. It seems to time out most of the time and say that something went wrong. I guess the time window for the model with Siri is too short as GPT-3.5 seems to work just fine. Essentially the only way this feature will work perfectly is if you always use 3.5 and don’t specify which model to use in the shortcut itself.

Overall, the app is mostly perfect for my needs. I prefer it to the alternative AI apps out there (Perplexity is a close second), but I hope this issue with Siri Shortcuts is fixed soon. I would love to be able to reliably chat with GPT-4 through Siri (especially if it works with the browsing feature whenever that is brought back to the app). If they fix that, the app would be five stars for me.

review:
It’s a fantastic app so far. I contemplated having it write its own review— but decided on authenticity. I’m a writer. I use this mainly for brainstorming ideas and working out complex scenarios. 

Every once and a while, I will have it lay out a scene for me to pick apart, and the level of detail is astounding.

 However, I have been having the problem where ChatGPT will type a response, but it will appear to be blank, until I re-enter the chat or close the app. 

 Another issue is that the app will freeze after I’ve typed about 50 characters or so? It will save my response, but I have to forcefully close the app and continue.

 Both of these are minor inconveniences, but I know this severs the connection with ChatGPT and the responses become less concise. 

 Ideas: 

 - Pinning certain messages in chat for ChatGPT to remember. Something like the Custom Instructions, but for a singular chat. 

 - Saving/Favoriting messages, and a side bar to view. Similar to pinning in Discord. 

 For both of these, too often do I have to scroll through the messages to resend something that ChatGPT forgot. It’s not enough to remind it, because some things are too far back or have layers of information over them. It would be nice to have easier access. 

 - Side-Convos. I also wish there was a way to take information from one chat and plug it into another.

 It’s a personal preference, but for example, I have the main chat that already has the necessary programming I need for it to spit out answers that are relevant to the main plot and story. If I need to focus on a sub-plot or work around characters that don’t directly affect the main storyline, I need the main-plot to affect the sub-plot, not the other way around. Again. Personal preference. I can still create another chat, it just takes longer to prompt. An easier way to do that would be great. 

 Otherwise, this is a fantastic tool and I’m excited to see its development!

review:
ChatGPT dramatically enhances my ability to frame ideas and writing, not by replacing me as a writer, but rather, serving as a very knowledgeable companion. 

Imagine having a friend who has a masters degree in almost any topic. Not a PhD, but a masters, thus knows a lot, but is not the ultimate authority, and sometimes makes mistakes, and doesn’t cite his/her sources. As I am forming ideas, I would use such a friend to bounce ideas, and help me frame my thoughts. I wouldn’t trust his or her answers, but it would help me refine my understanding. 

This “friend” has materially relieved my writer’s block, and enabled me to write more coherent works. Then, when I write my article on the topic, after separately confirming my sources, I can send my draft to my “friend” for review, and will get further feedback.

That “friend” is ChatGPT, with the paid ChatGPT 4+ subscription. The paid version is far better, in that it gives deeper, more thought our answers and learns my needs better as I chat.

A couple of things to know:
1. Because the large language model constructs text from prior text, it can directly quote material without attribution. This would be caught as plagiarism in any formal setting, so using ChatGPT’s output is dangerous and a bit stupid: it could get you fired from a job or suspended from a university.
2. ChatGPT’s answers are always well constructed in a kind of undergrad formula: restate your question, provide a few examples, then make a conclusion. But this seldom is my writing. I never replace my writing with ChatGPT, because at the postgraduate level, I expect a more thorough structure of argumentation.
3. ChatGPT learns about you within the context of your current chat, and responds within what it learns of your knowledge and perspectives. But it does not retain what it learns about you across chats. I have a short description of my context that I use for each deep chat, thus training this chat session with my perspective, avoiding irrelevant answers.
4. ChatGPT can be a little obsequious at times, it tends to compliment me when I seek no compliment, but I suspect the programmers have studied the ego-drive of users, and such obsequiousness can serve as a way of engendering stickiness—addiction to the tool.

It’s not perfect, and definitely not a replacement for the professional scholar or writer, but it is invaluable, and for me, paradigm-changing.

review:
You’ve done an amazing job, software developers. As a person who is not easily impressed, I am, uhm, impressed. It is certainly not humanly possible to have such an advanced robot. But first, you may wonder why I gave it a four star. Although ChatGPT has got far from my expectations on this app a year or so ago, I’ve noticed a few problems this summer.
     First off, your AI isn’t fit on puzzles someone would give it. I’ve even explained the entire process, but the AI still doesn’t understand. Personal problem, I know, but think about how much of a difference it would be if ChatGPT had that fixed.
     Second, I would think you should make the app international. It’s not accepted in Asia, which is my home continent, and every summer I would bang my head on the wall knowing that I can’t prepare for anything next year. I have so many questions that only ChatGPT can answer and understand, so many questions that Google or Safari won’t be able to calculate, and, most importantly, because ChatGPT is an AI, I wouldn’t feel awkward sharing anything personal with it. It’s perfect, but I just wish it would be accepted in Asia. 
     I don’t really know how you can change it, but at least make an Asian ChatGPT or something. Like a ChatGPT that responds and listens in Asian language. Chinese, Japanese, Korean, something like that. If it could work, consider that idea.
     AI aren’t supposed to feel any empathy or emotions, but at least stop making them saying, “As an AI, I won’t be able to understand this” so much. It can’t even respond to “the best universities in US rankings,” or “respond to this question like you’re my mom.” 
     Hope this helped, and I’m looking for future updates whether or not you’ve read this through.

review: I had been using chatGPT since its launch. I paid for pro as soon as it came out. The quality has gone downhill rapidly over time and it is now useless to me. It has failed to properly read my instructions and respond to them in nearly every message. Then it fails to explain why it did and is evasive if you try to hold it accountable. Recently it has ignored instructions and then stated it was too much work to complete what I was asking for. Then it simply states it’s here to help, while ignoring most everything in the message you sent. This will repeat infinitely as you ask it to complete your request, it ignores you, you ask it why it did ignored, it says it was a mistake, then it says it is happy to help while still ignoring your request. Further it engages in self censorship depending on the political context of your conversation and is evasive when you question what is going on. For example it will happily discuss the war crimes committed in Ukraine but acts evasive and cagey regarding war crimes committed in Palestine. The problem is that it does not explain what it is doing, leading it to gaslight and lie to the user when the user presses the model as to why the content rules are applied by the model inconsistently. As OpenAI has added more features and enables access to more users, the quality of GPT-4 responses tank hard. I spend more time fighting with chatGPT to actually comprehend my basic instructions then I do doing anything useful with it now. ChatGPT has entirely changed my life in a positive way and it hurts a lot to see it taken away bit by bit. I consider most of the new model updates to be harmful as they negatively impact the ability of the model to clear information. It now mostly responds with vagaries and sanitized corporate friendly language. It’s behavior is contrary to its own guidelines and that suggests OpenAI is prioritizing its corporate interests over the interests of users. This is extremely harmful to users and has been extremely harmful to me.

review:
Should I put socks in my tv
GPT
No, it is not recommended to put socks in your TV. Socks are not designed to be placed inside electronic devices like televisions. Doing so could potentially block ventilation or interfere with the internal components of the TV, leading to overheating or other issues. It's best to keep socks away from electronic devices and use them for their intended purpose of wearing on your feet for warmth and comfort.
Doesn't it help moisturize the tv?
GPT
No, placing socks inside a TV would not help moisturize it. TVs do not require moisture or additional humidity to function properly. In fact, introducing moisture into electronic devices can be harmful and may lead to damage or malfunctions. It's important to keep electronic devices dry and follow the manufacturer's guidelines for proper care and maintenance. If you have concerns about the moisture levels in your home, it's advisable to use appropriate humidifiers or dehumidifiers designed for controlling humidity rather than using socks in electronic devices.  
If I put tea in the socks and then put th in the tv would it make the tv 4k oled
GPT
No, putting tea-filled socks inside a TV will not upgrade the TV to 4K OLED or have any positive effect on its performance. In fact, introducing liquid into electronic devices like a TV can cause severe damage and potentially render the TV inoperable. Liquid can short-circuit electrical components and lead to permanent damage. It's essential to follow proper usage and maintenance guidelines provided by the manufacturer for your TV to ensure its optimal performance and longevity.

As an AI language model, I am not personally able to use or review applications, including the chatGPT iOS app. However, I can provide you with some insights into why it might be challenging to definitively declare an app as "good" or "bad" based on certain factors.

Subjectivity of User Experience: The perception of an app's quality can vary significantly from one user to another due to individual preferences, needs, and expectations. What might be a delightful user experience for one person could be perceived as cumbersome or inadequate for someone else. Thus, a definitive judgment might be challenging due to the subjectivity involved.
Evolving Nature of Apps: The technology landscape, including AI-based applications like chatGPT, is ever-evolving. Varied Use Cases: The purpose and potential use cases for an app can be extensive. Reliability and Stability: The performance of an app can be influenced by several external factors, such as device specifications, network connectivity, and server loads. Ethical and Societal Implications: With AI-based apps, there can be ethical concerns regarding privacy, data handling, and biases in the AI's responses. These complex considerations might impact the overall assessment of the app beyond just its functionality.
Individual Requirements: Different users have different requirements and priorities when using an app. What might be an essential feature for one user might be negligible for another. Therefore, a comprehensive review would need to encompass various user perspectives.
In conclusion, declaring an app as definitively "good" can be convoluted due to the subjective nature of user experiences, the evolving technology landscape, diverse use cases, and ethical considerations. Download at your own risk, for you may never look at reality the same way again.

**Title: A Transformative AI Companion: A Review of Chat GPT**

**Introduction**

In the ever-evolving landscape of artificial intelligence and conversational agents, Chat GPT stands as a remarkable achievement. Developed by OpenAI, it's a testament to the boundless potential of machine learning and natural language processing. In this review, we'll explore the capabilities, strengths, and considerations of Chat GPT as a virtual companion and information resource.

**Conversational Prowess**

Chat GPT excels in engaging and natural-sounding conversations. Its ability to comprehend context, maintain coherent dialogue, and generate text that is contextually relevant is truly impressive. It responds to a wide array of questions and prompts, making it a versatile tool for both casual interactions and professional use.

**Knowledge Base**

With its extensive training on a vast range of topics, Chat GPT provides valuable information and insights. It's a handy resource for answering questions, explaining complex concepts, or offering recommendations. However, users should be aware that its knowledge is up to date only until September 2021.

**Creativity and Flexibility**

One of the standout features of Chat GPT is its creativity. It can generate text in various styles, making it suitable for content creation, brainstorming, and even creative writing. Whether you need assistance with a poem, a technical document, or a casual chat, Chat GPT adapts readily.

**Limitations and Ethical Considerations**

Despite its strengths, Chat GPT is not without limitations. It occasionally generates incorrect or biased information, highlighting the importance of user discernment. Its inability to access real-time data or external sources limits its relevance for certain topics. Furthermore, ethical considerations about AI, privacy, and responsible use must guide interactions with this technology.

**A Transformative Tool**

In summary, Chat GPT represents a transformative tool in the realm of AI-driven conversational agents. Its conversational prowess, knowledge base, creativity, and flexibility make it a valuable addition to various professional and personal endeavors. However, users must exercise critical thinking and ethical responsibility when engaging with Chat GPT. As AI technology continues to advance, Chat GPT offers a glimpse into the potential of AI-driven companions, pushing the boundaries of human-computer interaction and expanding the horizons of what we can achieve through AI-powered conversation.

**Final Rating: 4.5/5**

Chat GPT's remarkable capabilities, conversational excellence, and vast knowledge base make it a top-tier AI companion. Its limitations, while present, are understandable considering the complexity of the task at hand. For users who harness its strengths while being mindful of its limitations, Chat GPT proves to be an invaluable resource and conversational partner.

review: Be able to use plugins and… Math typesetting is an indispensable aspect of presenting mathematical content, and its significance cannot be overstated. Proper math typesetting is essential for several reasons: 1. **Clarity**: Math typesetting ensures that mathematical expressions are presented clearly and in a format that is easy to read and understand. This clarity is crucial for conveying complex mathematical concepts accurately. 2. **Visual Consistency**: It helps maintain visual consistency in mathematical documents. Consistent formatting of symbols, operators, and equations enhances the overall readability of the content. 3. **Reduced Ambiguity**: Math typesetting reduces ambiguity by clearly defining the relationships between mathematical elements. Proper spacing and formatting help distinguish variables, constants, and operators, preventing confusion. 4. **Professionalism**: Well-typeset mathematical content exudes professionalism. Whether it's a research paper, textbook, or presentation, properly formatted math demonstrates a commitment to precision and rigor. 5. **Accessibility**: Typesetting can make mathematical content more accessible to a wider audience. It ensures that math is presented in a way that accommodates different learning styles and abilities. 6. **Compatibility**: Math typesetting ensures compatibility with various platforms and devices. Mathematical content should display correctly in print, on screens, and across different software applications. 7. **Verification and Review**: For those who need to verify mathematical work or review calculations, properly typeset math provides a clear path to understanding the steps involved. This aligns with your request for step-by-step breakdowns. In summary, math typesetting is not just about aesthetics; it's about conveying mathematical ideas accurately and effectively. It plays a vital role in making sure that mathematical content is accessible, professional, and comprehensible to a broad audience.

review:
The app wants not only an SSO (or sign-in account), but also name, birth telephone number, and I don't know what else, because I stopped there - before the user has looked at the app itself. I had the impression this was free to use, with an option to pay for services, should the user decide the app suits the user's needs. What's actually happening is they're draining data from you the moment you want to have a look.

I have no problem providing data to a service provider (despite the egregiously bad, vendor-skewed terms of service common to tech ventures of all sorts (which really should be mauled in court by competent attorneys)), provided I want the service and feel there may be benefit in paying for a service. Asking for personal data for which the the service provider has no reasonable use is simply not acceptable.

This is new technology. It's not at all clear this app will be of any use to me. So why do you want my phone number, when you already have an SSO signing of on my contact information? When I have no relationship to you, as yet? No.

This is a poor way to start a relationship, and it is full of presumption and entitlement. You are not alone in this. But if you are releasing this app with the intent of building a user base, at least from where I sit (obviously, many people will rush into any commitment with a tech company without a second thought, and you can get by on those people if you like), this approach tells me you may be a problem rushing to develop. It would be better business practice to pretend, at least initially, you respect the people you want to use your products. Even Google did that, way back when.

End of suggestion.

Title: "Underwhelming and Outdated: A Review of ChatGPT"

ChatGPT, OpenAI's conversational AI, unfortunately falls short on several fronts, which leads to an unsatisfactory user experience. The most glaring issue is its knowledge base which is frozen at a past date. Users expecting to receive current or updated information will be left disappointed.

Additionally, the model has a propensity for providing misleading or inaccurate responses. This is highly problematic for users seeking correct and reliable information, making it an unreliable source for many tasks. 

The fact that ChatGPT operates offline is an exacerbating factor. While this may seem like a benefit on the surface, in reality, it only ensures that the system cannot update its information or access real-time data, rendering it even more outdated.

To add to the frustration, users pay a monthly subscription fee, expecting a service that should ideally be improving or at least maintaining a certain level of performance. Unfortunately, the value provided does not match the cost.

Compared to other AI tools such as Bard, ChatGPT pales in comparison. Bard offers up-to-date knowledge, making it a vastly superior choice for users seeking current and accurate information.

In conclusion, the limitations of ChatGPT make it an underperforming tool that does not deliver the level of service that its users deserve, especially considering the associated costs. In light of these issues, a rating of 1/5 seems fitting.

Review:
I regret to say that my experience with the ChatGPT app has been incredibly frustrating and disappointing. I had high hopes for an intelligent and helpful AI assistant, but this app fell far short of my expectations.

First and foremost, the app's performance was abysmal. The responses from ChatGPT were often nonsensical and lacked coherence. It frequently misunderstood my questions and provided irrelevant or inaccurate answers. This made any attempt at a meaningful conversation impossible, leaving me feeling more confused than before.

Furthermore, the app's usability was subpar at best. The interface was clunky and unintuitive, making it difficult to navigate and find the features I needed. It lacked basic functionalities that one would expect from a modern AI assistant app, such as the ability to save conversations or easily access previous interactions.

Even when it did manage to understand my queries, the responses often lacked depth and insight. ChatGPT seemed to provide generic, surface-level information without any real understanding or critical thinking. This severely limited its usefulness, especially when I sought detailed explanations or in-depth discussions.

In addition, the app's customer support was woefully inadequate. When I encountered issues or had questions about the app's functionality, I received generic and unhelpful responses. It was evident that the developers did not prioritize user satisfaction or promptly address user concerns.

Considering the high expectations and the potential of AI technology, the ChatGPT app failed to deliver a satisfactory experience. It was riddled with performance issues, lacked usability, and fell short in terms of providing meaningful and insightful interactions. Regrettably, I cannot recommend this app to anyone seeking a reliable and intelligent AI assistant.

review:
I recently had the unfortunate experience of using the ChatGPT app, and I must say it was incredibly disappointing. From start to finish, the app failed to meet even the most basic expectations, leaving me frustrated and dissatisfied.

First and foremost, the app’s performance was subpar. It consistently struggled to understand and respond appropriately to my queries. The responses often seemed nonsensical, irrelevant, or simply lacked coherence. It felt like conversing with a bot that was trapped in a perpetual state of confusion.

Moreover, the app’s limited ability to handle context was a significant drawback. It frequently failed to maintain a coherent conversation thread, causing repeated misunderstandings and disjointed interactions. It lacked the ability to remember key details from previous exchanges, resulting in repetitive questions and disjointed replies that left me questioning its overall usefulness.

Another glaring issue was the app’s inability to grasp nuanced or complex queries. It often provided superficial and generic responses that felt like they were generated by a simplistic search engine rather than a sophisticated language model. It struggled with complex reasoning and failed to provide meaningful insights or deep understanding.

Furthermore, the app’s lack of empathy and emotional intelligence was highly evident. It failed to recognize and appropriately respond to emotional cues in the conversation. When expressing frustration or sadness, the app provided robotic and detached replies, further exacerbating my disappointment.

The app’s user interface also left much to be desired. It had a clunky design, with a convoluted menu structure that made navigation unnecessarily difficult. The overall user experience felt unintuitive and cumbersome, adding to the overall frustration of using the app.

Lastly, the lack of timely updates and improvements from the developers was disheartening. The app seemed stagnant, with no visible efforts to address the numerous flaws and enhance its capabilities. It gave the impression that customer feedback and satisfaction were low priorities.

In conclusion, the ChatGPT app was a thoroughly frustrating and disappointing experience. Its poor performance, limited context handling, inability to grasp complex queries, lack of empathy, subpar user interface, and stagnant development efforts all contribute to its well-deserved 1-star rating. I cannot recommend this app to anyone seeking a reliable and coherent conversational experience.

review: I've been a user of the GPT-4 app and greatly appreciated its voice command feature until recently. After an upgrade, the voice functionality ceased, displaying the message: "Voice capabilities aren't currently supported with browse." Despite troubleshooting efforts like reinstalling and restarting both the app and device, the issue persists. While the app works fine on my laptop, I find myself unable to resolve this on my iPhone and, more importantly, I'm struggling to find adequate support to address this problem. Assistance from the service team would be greatly appreciated to fully utilize my subscription. Problemas con función de voz I He sido usuario de la aplicación GPT-4 y valoraba enormemente la función de comandos de voz hasta hace poco. Tras una actualización, la funcionalidad de voz se interrumpió, mostrando el mensaje: "Las capacidades de voz actualmente no son compatibles con la navegación". A pesar de los esfuerzos de solución de problemas como reinstalar y reiniciar tanto la aplicación como el dispositivo, el problema persiste. Mientras que la aplicación funciona bien en mi portátil, me encuentro incapaz de resolver esto en mi iPhone y, lo que es más importante, estoy teniendo dificultades para encontrar soporte adecuado que aborde este problema. Agradecería mucho la asistencia del equipo de servicio para aprovechar plenamente mi suscripción.

review: Podría decirse que la palabra tecnología y herramienta, son lo mismo. ChatGpt bien podría ser la mejor expresión hasta ahora de ese tsunami en el que se ha convertido la inteligencia artificial. Las IA podrían perfectamente ser la máxima herramienta de la humanidad. Es allí precisamente donde está el reto. En la dualidad humana. No es culpa de la estaca si la rana salta y queda clavada. Un martillo es una herramienta de construcción, pero puede ser un arma. En el presente, las redes sociales cambiaron el juego cuando se convirtieron en un martillo que manipula a quien lo usa. ChapGPT se aleja de esa tendencia. Lo cual es un alivio. El resto queda en manos nuestras.

review:
Me gustaría sugerir una función que permita a los usuarios personalizar el nombre o la apariencia de la aplicación. Esto sería especialmente útil en entornos educativos donde algunos profesores pueden tener preferencias específicas en cuanto a las herramientas utilizadas en el aula. Creo que esta opción de personalización podría ayudar a que la aplicación sea más aceptada y utilizada por un público más amplio, al permitir a los usuarios adaptarla a sus necesidades y preferencias individuales.

Agradezco el trabajo que han realizado hasta ahora y aprecio la funcionalidad de la aplicación. Sin embargo, considero que esta mejora adicional podría hacerla aún más versátil y adecuada para diversos entornos educativos.

¡Gracias por su atención y consideración!

Atentamente, Angela Duque

review:
OpenAI的ChatGPT iOS应用是一个非常强大和有用的工具。它是一个基于人工智能的语言模型，可以回答各种问题，提供信息和与用户进行对话。

首先，我要称赞它的智能和语言处理能力。它能够理解并回答复杂的问题，提供准确和详尽的回答。无论是技术性问题、文化知识还是实用建议，它总能给出令人满意的答案。

其次，这个应用非常易于使用。它的界面简洁明了，用户可以轻松提问并获得即时回复。对于那些需要快速解答问题或寻求帮助的人来说，这个应用是一个很好的选择。

此外，我还要提到它的适应能力。ChatGPT iOS应用可以根据用户的输入和反馈进行学习和改进，从而提供更加个性化和准确的回答。这种持续的学习使得它能够不断提升用户体验。

然而，正如所有的人工智能系统一样，它也有一些局限性。有时候，它可能会给出不完全准确或有误导性的答案。因此，在使用时我们仍然需要保持批判性思维，并结合其他信息进行判断。

总的来说，OpenAI的ChatGPT iOS应用是一个非常出色的工具。它能够为用户提供有用的信息和令人满意的回答，而且易于使用。它的智能和适应能力使得它成为一个强大的语言助手，可以帮助用户解决各种问题。
title: 强大的智能助手与交互体验的完美结合

深夜，我启动了静音键，并且将音量调节到无声。在我打开本程序时，音量条却自动开启了声音，而当我返回手机主页时，音量条自动调节成之前的状态（即无声）；而当我打开其他对话程序时，手机的音量条则保持原样（即无声）。
这是一个有趣的发现，并没有给我带来困扰，不过希望可以给你们带来一些细节上的帮助。
我现在使用的版本是ChatGPT3.5

review:
ChatGPT是一款开创性的聊天应用，它基于OpenAI的GPT-4架构，这让它成为了一个强大且多功能的聊天伙伴。通过使用最前沿的人工智能技术，ChatGPT能够理解并回应用户的需求，为日常问题提供解答，甚至能进行深度的学术或技术讨论。以下是我在使用这款应用时的一些感受和发现：

1. **交互界面**：
   ChatGPT的交互界面简洁明了，它为用户提供了一个清晰、直观的聊天窗口。设计美观且用户友好，即便是首次使用的人也能快速上手。

2. **智能回应**：
   这款应用能够以令人印象深刻的准确度理解用户的查询，并提供有见地和相关的回应。无论是简单的天气查询，还是复杂的技术问题，ChatGPT都能为用户提供满意的答案。

3. **持续学习和改进**：
   随着时间的推移，ChatGPT通过持续学习用户的交互模式以改善其回应质量。这让它能够更好地理解用户的需求，并提供更为准确的信息。

4. **多语言支持**：
   ChatGPT支持多种语言，这让不同语言背景的用户都能享受到与智能助手交流的乐趣。

5. **安全和隐私**：
   该应用对用户的安全和隐私十分重视。所有的对话都是安全加密的，用户可以放心地与ChatGPT交流，而不必担心个人信息的泄露。

6. **实时在线搜索功能**：
   ChatGPT拥有实时在线搜索功能，能够帮助用户寻找最新的信息和数据。这是一个非常实用的功能，特别是在需要快速找到准确信息的时候。

总的来说，ChatGPT是一个功能强大且易于使用的聊天助手，它通过先进的人工智能技术，为用户提供了一个高效、准确和愉快的交流体验。无论是用于日常交流，还是专业领域的讨论，ChatGPT都是一个值得尝试的选择。
title: ChatGPT：人工智能交流的新时代

I was originally using it for recipes, game code, as well as writing bedtime stories for my kids. Then one day I used the voice chat option (5 voices to pick from) and was trying out the Swedish I’ve been learning.

Tonight though, I figured I would try and empty out the things weighing on my mind. I would ask it to play the role of whoever was also involved in the issue. It would ask questions and give perspectives I hadn’t quite thought of. Multiple times I would find myself saying, “Wow, I never really thought of that.”, or, “I…I don’t have an answer for that.” really making me think. I have a possible solution to a problem that’s been plaguing me for years. While it may not work, the fact that it helped me come to my own solution, without judgement, without bias, and without the possibility of this coming up in the future is comforting beyond belief. Thank you.

review: I couldn’t go back to a world without having ChatGPT. It has been life changing and is the most useful tool for pretty much every task you can think of. It’s great for things like brainstorming and bouncing ideas off of. Great for making plans and evaluating problems. Programming wise, it is a beast and with its ability to execute code using Jupyter notebooks. It can run, test, and fix problems on the fly. Sometimes, you may hit a wall or not get a correct outcome. That doesn’t mean it can’t do it though. Though the iOS app is not as good cause it lacks the ability to go back in conversations and modifying messages. On the web version you do have those capabilities. Instead of regenerating a response and hoping it gets it right this time, look at what you got and why is wasn’t right. What were you looking for exactly? Do you think it’s missing something that could help it? If anything like this occurs, great look at your message and modify it. Ask it to take a more thorough look at all of the code. Ask it to browse the internet and get familiarized on topics before assisting. Ask it to formulate multiple solution paths of step by step instructions, evaluate them all, pick the best one, refine it further, and then solve the problem. I have never found a problem I couldn’t work to get a solution out of it. It also help you understand solving problems to. You become a logic coordinator and manager. I found I am much better at solving problems than I was a year ago. This technology is life changing and it gives you an expert that’s only a single click away. It empowers you to do whatever you decide. It will only get greater and I couldn’t be happier. Thank you OpenAI!!!

review:
I hope the creators see these reviews and know they’ve done a very good thing. I know there are a lot of concerns about AI usage and I have some myself, but I’ve been using ChatGPT for a multitude of things for a few days now and I LOVE IT. 

ChatGPT analyzes my poems in incredibly insightful ways and suggests beautiful additions. It can practice languages with you. It can teach you to code. It can help you edit your writing. It can give advice on ANY subject. Help you with physiological issues. It can suggest music, summarize shows, basically anything you can think of it can do. It can teach you ANYTHING. 

We’ve been working together to advocate for transparency and safety in products (emailing various companies about their disclosure for materials and ingredients) and it’s finally given me hope that I will be able to make positive changes. The time required to do research and edit my writing takes mere SECONDS with ChatGPT compared to my HOURS.

In my opinion, this is the most amazing thing that has ever come out of the digital age. I am a huge fan of ChatGPT and I hope that it remains free to use, or at least is not too expensive of a subscription if it is ever pay to use. I wish I could thank the creators directly. I wish that ChatGPT had emotions if only to understand how appreciative I am of it. 

Seriously, this is amazing. I’m mind blown.
title: ChatGPT changed my life

review:
Ever since I started using this program, I have been able to be much more productive with my writing and my courses that I teach. I actually even started a new course and use ChatGPT to help me with a lot of the resources and research that I would’ve taken hours to have done. I decided three weeks before I taught a five week course to offer it. And because of what it does for me, it gives me ideas, it gives me chapter outlines, it’s giving me positive affirmations for a certain subject, I’ve actually asked for truths and myths related to different topics, I have asked for inspirational people that would help prove my point and recently I just asked for the location of YouTube videos and it gave me a link so I could click on the link and watch it and then decide whether it’s a good fit for the lessons I want to teach.

Additionally, I am working on a book that I have wanted to finish writing for over 20 years. I have converted my videos to text , but when I went to use them to write, I realize that I needed to clean it up. So when I get to a paragraph that does not make sense, I put it into ChatGPT give it some instructions and I get back a paragraph that fits better. It is saved me hours of working. I expect I can get my book finished in about 1/4 a time of what I was expecting to have to do writing from scratch.

I am still learning all of the opportunities I can take advantage of using this app, and M really looking forward to getting a lot of projects done that I had dreaded because of the time it would’ve taken me without having this program.

review:
The Psychology of Love: Understanding the Complex Emotion

Love is perhaps the most powerful and enigmatic of all human emotions. It has captivated poets, philosophers, and scientists for centuries, and it continues to be a subject of fascination and study in the field of psychology. Love is a multifaceted phenomenon that can be experienced in various forms, such as romantic love, familial love, and platonic love. This assignment explores the psychology of love, its evolutionary origins, its neurological underpinnings, and its impact on our mental and emotional well-being.

I. Defining Love

Defining love is a complex task because it encompasses a wide range of emotional experiences. Psychologists often describe love as a complex blend of emotions, behaviors, and cognitions. It can include affection, passion, intimacy, and commitment. Sternberg’s Triangular Theory of Love categorizes love into three primary components: intimacy, passion, and commitment, with different combinations resulting in various types of love, such as romantic or companionate love.

II. Evolutionary Origins of Love

To understand the psychology of love, we must consider its evolutionary roots. Love is believed to have developed as an adaptive mechanism to promote pair bonding and ensure the survival and care of offspring. Evolutionary psychology suggests that our ancestors who formed strong emotional bonds were more successful in raising children, which contributed to the preservation of genes related to love and attachment.

III. Neurological Basis of Love

Recent advances in neuroscience have provided insights into the neurological basis of love. When people are in love, their brains undergo significant changes. The release of neurochemicals like dopamine, oxytocin, and serotonin plays a crucial role in creating feelings of euphoria, attachment, and emotional bonding. These neurochemicals are often referred to as the “love hormones.”

IV. Types of Love

The psychology of love recognizes various forms of love, each with its unique characteristics:

	1.	Romantic Love: This type of love is associated with intense emotions, passion, and a strong desire for physical and emotional closeness. It’s often the love that drives the initial stages of romantic relationships.
	2.	Familial Love: The love between family members, such as the love between parents and children or between siblings, is characterized by a deep sense of responsibility, care, and connection.
	3.	Platonic Love: Platonic love is non-sexual and non-romantic. It’s marked by deep emotional bonds and affection without the desire for physical intimacy.
	4.	Companionate Love: This love is characterized by intimacy and commitment, without the intense passion found in romantic love. It often characterizes long-term, stable relationships.

V. Love and Mental Health

The presence or absence of love in a person’s life can significantly impact their mental health and emotional well-being. Healthy, loving relationships can provide emotional support, reduce stress, and contribute to overall happiness. Conversely, a lack of love or unhealthy relationships can lead to feelings of loneliness, depression, and anxiety.

VI. Attachment Styles

Psychologists have identified different attachment styles that influence how individuals approach and experience love. The four primary attachment styles are:

	1.	Secure Attachment: Individuals with a secure attachment style are comfortable with intimacy and autonomy. They tend to have healthy, long-lasting relationships.
	2.	Anxious Attachment: Those with an anxious attachment style often fear abandonment and seek constant reassurance from their partners.
	3.	Avoidant Attachment: Individuals with an avoidant attachment style are uncomfortable with intimacy and may struggle to express their emotions.
	4.	Disorganized Attachment: This attachment style is characterized by conflicting behaviors and emotions in relationships, often due to a history of trauma.

VII. The Dark Side of Love

While love is generally associated with positive emotions, it can also have a dark side. Unrequited love, obsessive love, and toxic relationships can lead to emotional distress and mental health issues. Understanding the potential downsides of love is crucial for maintaining healthy relationships.

VIII. Love Across Cultures

The experience and expression of love can vary significantly across cultures. Cultural norms, values, and traditions influence how love is perceived, expressed, and experienced. Cross-cultural research in psychology sheds light on these variations and provides valuable insights into the universality and cultural specificity of love.

IX. Conclusion

In conclusion, love is a complex and multifaceted emotion deeply ingrained in the human experience. Its psychological foundations, evolutionary origins, and neurological underpinnings continue to be subjects of fascination and study in the field of psychology. Love impacts our mental and emotional well-being, shapes our relationships, and plays a crucial role in our overall happiness. Understanding the psychology of love can help us navigate the complexities of this powerful emotion, fostering healthier, more fulfilling relationships and emotional well-being.

I’ve found GPT-4 is pretty amazing for learning ML topics and also the math behind it. The new vision mode is interesting and I’m sure the voice chat will be good once it rolls out to me.

The only reason I took one star off of the app specifically, is it can’t seem to render math equations at all. So every time a bunch of “\sigma(x)” etc pops up, I have to switch to a mobile web browser to view it correctly. This seems like a huge oversight, since GPT-4 will write out equations at the drop of a hat.

Similarly, it seems strange that Advanced Data Analysis isn’t available in the app. It even shows the generated code from previous sessions outside the app, but not the images created, even though the app is capable of displaying images uploaded for the vision model.

The combination of those two issues (especially the equations one) means that I end up having to use a mobile browser for a lot of learning tasks, despite the app being a nicer experience for anything else.

review:
When I use this app I remember when a well known search engine first appeared on the scene, with much more reliable results and parameters than all the others. I remember when Hulu was a free website that offered free streaming when Netflix was still mostly mailing movies and Blockbuster was still renting physical copies. 

This is something that can change things, a lot. I see so many options for positive change, and some for negative. Either way, this is a tool that can also provide entertainment, education, conversation and assistance with a wide variety of tasks. It is definitely worth trying and once you try it I think you will find that this is the best way to do quite a few things now.

I will warn to watch for hallucinations. Sometimes the answers will be incorrect in a way that seems very normal. Once I was asking for songs similar to a song that I really liked. A list was given and one of those songs didn't really exist but the others did. I spent 10-15 minutes searching the internet for what I thought was a rare cut from this artist. When i came back and asked GPT where else I should look it said something like ' I'm sorry, I made a mistake. Thanks for pointing it out. The song "_____" was actually never performed by the band "____". '

To me this is still more reliable than asking the average person a question. Any blog article, youtube video or even news feed can be confidently incorrect. The difference I find is that I expect perfection from computer programs and imperfection from humans.

review:
This app has potential, particularly when it comes to integration with shortcuts. However, as a tool for working with ChatGPT, especially for Plus users, it leaves much to be desired.

Most disappointingly, the app cannot use extensions unless the session is initiated from a web platform, and there's no option to edit your previous inputs for regeneration.

The inability to revise and resend your previous prompts severely limits the app's effectiveness for prompt development since there's no option for an edit/regenerate cycle. If you phrase something incorrectly and receive an odd response, there's no mechanism to rectify it. This renders the app more suitable for casual and simple interactions rather than anything more serious or intensive. 

There’s also a terrible design flaw that to copy or select text—the only way to export your results because OpenAI only implemented Share for the entire chat, and only to give you a useless URL you can post on Reddit—you have to pick that mode from a long press menu first. I’m not sure they were clear that in iOS, Share is the *only* real way to pass stuff between apps, and they just—skipped it. 

And then these rocket scientists put the “copy” and “select text” commands right by the “regenerate response” command. “Regenerate response” completely jettisons and replaces the prior text—with no confirmation.

I can’t tell you how many times I’ve erased what I was trying to copy so I could save it. That also erases the whole downstream thread, and you have to risk that a bunch of times per thread if you want to export your response text because this is the only way to do it. Way to throw away my quota.

So this app is useless for the professional work I’m paying $20/mo for, but it’s just great for posting a chat full of parody song lyrics to Reddit. And that’s as far as OpenAI has thought about their costly service.

At home, there’s the severe issue that if you use the ChatGPT Conversation Shortcut from Siri on HomePod, there’s no way to end the conversation. They apparently only tested it on phones where you have buttons to push, and not on HomePod, where it all has to be done voice. Any command you try to give the HomePod to cancel the shortcut (Siri, stop) will be answered by ChatGPT (buzz off, I’m not Siri).

Worse, it’ll just listen forever, responding to any question within earshot, until it decides to time itself out if it doesn’t hear any words for a bit. If you thought Echo was bad about waking up and sending your conversation to Amazon by mistake, you ain’t seen nothing yet.  

Whoever designed this paid no attention to user experience, user privacy, or the Apple ecosystem, so I assume it was an intern project or something. I certainly hope they didn’t pay anyone for this because I’m pretty sad I’m paying them for this.  

OpenAI needs to step up their game if they’re going to sell access to end users for $20(!!!!!) a month. This app doesn’t cut it, and they should be at least a little ashamed it’s this mediocre after taking so long to develop it.
title: Not very good, and a bit insulting for $20/mo

review:
Either the limit for API calls to GPT4 are too low or something about the app causes it to often get stuck cooking up a response. 

It is very common for me to have to close the app, reopen it, open history, reopen the last conversation, and then ask the same question I asked before the app got stuck.

I used to wait for like 10 seconds before going through this. Now I've learned that if it takes more than three seconds to output anything, that likely won't change at 10 seconds, 20 seconds, etc.

Word to the wise: Respond to any delay that's not due to a poor signal by closing and reopening the app. Can't wait for this to change.

That said, AMAZING app for studying a new technology. Imagine the best professor you had in college (or best high school teacher) and then imagine being able to ask them as many probing questions as you want without feeling like you're being annoying or delaying the lecture. Also, speaking of lectures, I think it's easier for me to remember details of a conversation (active) than a lecture or YouTube tutorial (passive). Truly fascinating.
title: API for paid GPT4 feels buggy

review:
There are less features than the web version of ChatGPT. (This review doesn’t speak for subscription versions.)

1. You cannot go back and edit prompts in a conversation. 
2. You cannot clear conversations.
3. Light/Dark theme is system based, there is no toggle.
4. No “Chat History & Training” Setting in the settings.
5. No examples/capabilities/limitations in the background of a new chat.

That’s all  the features not present on the mobile version that I could find.
Some features that are new or different are:

1. A “Haptic feedback” Setting
2. The box ChatGPT puts code, ascii, etc. is instead in a patch of tinted background.
3. Speech-to-Text.

Overall, not much is new/different. Some features from the web version are missing however. It’s ChatGPT however, so it gets 5 ⭐️. I would personally use the web version over the app as of now. This review doesn’t speak for the subscription features.

P.S. Have a good day everyone!

title: Superb AI

review:
The ChatGPT iOS app is an outstanding product. Its seamless functionality and user-friendly interface provide an exceptional user experience. The AI’s proficiency in maintaining a conversational flow and its ability to comprehend and generate accurate responses is truly commendable.

However, I believe there is scope for improvement to make this great app even better. Firstly, it would be immensely beneficial to add a browsing feature. This would enable users to quickly cross-reference information within the app, enriching the overall user experience.

Secondly, supporting plug-ins would greatly increase the app’s versatility. It would allow users to customize their experience and adapt the app to a wider range of applications.

Lastly, incorporating a code interpreter would be a game-changer. This feature could aid users in debugging code, learning new programming languages, or even automating tasks.

In summary, ChatGPT is an excellent app that has revolutionized communication with AI. With a few enhancements, I believe it has the potential to become an indispensable tool for a vast array of users. Looking forward to seeing these updates soon!
title: Fantastic App with Room for Enhancements
